extractions.problem: Predicting chemotherapeutic response in patients using baseline tumor gene expression data, bridging in vitro cell line drug sensitivity to in vivo clinical outcomes.
extractions.method: Whole-genome ridge regression models fitted on gene expression vs drug IC50 in CGP cell lines, with ComBat batch correction, applied to baseline tumor expression from clinical trials.
extractions.finding: Cell line-derived ridge regression models predict in vivo drug response in 3 of 4 clinical trials, with performance comparable to or better than gene signatures derived directly from clinical data.
extractions.dataset: CGP cell line panel (~10,000 genes), docetaxel/bortezomib/cisplatin/erlotinib clinical trials
extractions.baseline: Gene signatures derived from clinical trial data, random forests, PAM, Lasso, ElasticNet
extractions.result: Predictions equally good or better than clinical-derived gene signatures in 3/4 trials

extractions.problem: Providing a freely accessible, comprehensive collection of small molecule crystal structures to support world-wide crystallographic collaboration.
extractions.method: Open-access database with web-based search (unit cell parameters, chemical composition, SMARTS/SMILES substructure), CIF format storage, and user-driven deposition platform.
extractions.finding: COD aggregated ~150,000 crystal structures with open access, enabling material identification via powder diffractograms, teaching, and grid computing applications.
extractions.result: ~150,000 structures collected, estimated ~800,000 total known structures

extractions.problem: Understanding how extracellular vesicles interact with peripheral blood mononuclear cells in whole blood to inform EV-based targeted drug delivery.
extractions.method: Ex vivo whole blood model using PalmGRET/MemGlow-labeled EVs spiked into macaque blood, quantified by flow cytometry and nanoluciferase assays across dose and time courses.
extractions.finding: EVs preferentially associate with CD20+ B cells in ex vivo whole blood at levels similar to in vivo, validating ex vivo blood as a reliable model for studying EV-blood cell interactions.
extractions.dataset: Pig-tailed macaque (Macaca nemestrina) whole blood, Expi293F- and U-87 MG-derived EVs
extractions.metric: % EV-positive cells by flow cytometry, nanoluciferase RLU normalized by total protein
extractions.baseline: In vivo intravenous EV administration in macaques
extractions.result: >60% CD20+ B cells associated with EVs, ex vivo levels similar to in vivo

extractions.problem: Developing a vaccine against Vibrio harveyi vibriosis to reduce mortality in marine aquaculture fish as an alternative to antibiotics.
extractions.method: Formalin-killed whole cell V. harveyi (FKVh) vaccine administered intraperitoneally with booster dose at week 2, evaluated by ELISA (IgM), lysozyme activity assays, and live challenge at 10^8 CFU/fish.
extractions.finding: FKVh vaccination significantly enhanced survival and immune response (IgM antibodies in serum, mucus, gut lavage and lysozyme activity) in marine red hybrid tilapia against vibriosis.
extractions.dataset: 180 marine red hybrid tilapia (Oreochromis niloticus × O. mossambicus), V. harveyi strain Vh1
extractions.metric: Relative percentage survival, IgM antibody titer (ELISA OD), lysozyme activity (U/mg)

extractions.problem: Creating a comprehensive, executable mechanistic model of the cell division cycle molecular network to enable genome-scale signaling models and eventually eukaryotic whole-cell models.
extractions.method: rxncon reaction-contingency language for genome-scale mechanistic modeling with parameter-free Boolean network simulation (BoolNet), validated by genotype-to-phenotype predictions at residue resolution.
extractions.finding: The cell division cycle control network comprises three distinct control circuits (G1/S, G2/M, M/G1) monitoring three independent replication cycles, rather than a single closed cycle as previously modeled.
extractions.dataset: Saccharomyces cerevisiae cell division cycle (357 components, 229 proteins, 790 reactions, 1238 states, 598 contingencies)
extractions.metric: Phenotype prediction accuracy (viable/inviable mutants at residue resolution)
extractions.baseline: Previous closed-cycle CDC models without explicit replication cycle dependence
extractions.result: 357 components, 790 reactions, 3 distinct control circuits identified

extractions.problem: Developing a computational framework for iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes that incorporates experimental variability to predict patient-specific cardiac arrhythmia vulnerability.
extractions.method: Whole-cell Hodgkin-Huxley model with single exponential voltage-dependent gating, parameterized to multiple experimental iPSC-CM datasets, with population-of-models variability analysis across 5 key currents.
extractions.finding: Experimentally-informed population of cellular models reproduces the full range of inter-subject variability observed in iPSC-CMs and links molecular mechanisms to immature vs mature phenotypes.
extractions.dataset: iPSC-CM experimental data from multiple laboratories (Ma et al., Jalife Lab — conventional and PDMS preparations)
extractions.metric: Action potential duration, calcium transient amplitude, spontaneous beating rate, maximum diastolic potential
extractions.baseline: ten Tusscher 2004 and Shannon 2004 cardiomyocyte model formulations
extractions.result: Population models predict robust inter-subject variability matching experimental iPSC-CM phenotypes

extractions.problem: Determining the frequency and functional importance of extrachromosomal DNA (ecDNA) amplification across human cancers.
extractions.method: Whole-genome sequencing, FISH, metaphase cytogenetic analysis of 2,572 cells across 17 cancer types, with ECdetect software for automated ecDNA detection and mathematical modeling of amplification dynamics.
extractions.finding: ecDNA is found in nearly half of human cancers but almost never in normal cells; driver oncogenes are most commonly amplified on ecDNA, increasing transcript levels and intratumoral heterogeneity.
extractions.dataset: 17 cancer types, 2,572 metaphase cells, NCI-60 cell line panel, patient-derived xenografts
extractions.metric: ecDNA frequency, oncogene copy number, transcript levels, intratumoral heterogeneity
extractions.result: ecDNA in ~50% of cancers, almost never in normal cells

extractions.problem: Understanding genomic diversity and mutational evolution within individual breast tumors at single-cell resolution.
extractions.method: Nuc-seq: single nucleus whole-genome and exome sequencing using G2/M nuclei, combined with single nuclei copy number profiling and targeted single-molecule sequencing for validation.
extractions.finding: Aneuploid rearrangements occurred early in punctuated bursts and remained stable during clonal expansion, while point mutations evolved gradually generating extensive clonal diversity; TNBC showed 13.3× increased mutation rate.
extractions.dataset: ER+ breast cancer and triple-negative ductal carcinoma (two patients), single tumor and normal nuclei
extractions.metric: Coverage breadth, mutation frequency, mutation rate
extractions.baseline: Normal cells from same patients, bulk tumor sequencing
extractions.result: 91% mean coverage breadth, 13.3× increased mutation rate in TNBC, diverse mutations at <10% frequency

extractions.problem: Understanding how chromosome conformation and organization change across the cell cycle at single-cell resolution in mammalian cells.
extractions.method: High-throughput single-cell Hi-C with Mbo I digestion, Tn5 tagmentation, and FACS sorting into 96-well plates, analyzing thousands of mouse ESCs with whole-genome 3D structural modeling.
extractions.finding: Chromosomal compartments, TADs, insulation and loops are governed by distinct cell-cycle dynamics; DNA replication builds compartments while reducing TAD insulation; loops remain stable from G1 through S/G2.
extractions.dataset: 2,924 F1 hybrid mouse ESCs (129 × Castaneus), 1,992 passed QC
extractions.metric: Contact frequency, trans-contact fraction, TAD insulation score, compartment strength
extractions.baseline: Bulk Hi-C maps, sorted M-phase cell Hi-C
extractions.result: 1,992 cells analyzed, median 127,233 distinct contacts/cell, 5.87% median trans-contacts

extractions.problem: Systematically identifying and characterizing E. coli K-12 genes that lack experimental evidence of function to support whole-cell modeling efforts.
extractions.method: Semi-automated workflow integrating annotations from EcoCyc, EcoGene, UniProt and RegulonDB with keyword-based categorization, consensus rules, and manual curation.
extractions.finding: 1,600 of 4,623 E. coli genes (34.6%) lack experimental evidence of function; y-ome genes have lower expression, are enriched in the chromosome termination region, and most often encode membrane proteins and transporters.
extractions.dataset: E. coli K-12 MG1655 genome (4,623 unique genes across 4 knowledge bases)
extractions.metric: Gene count, percentage of genome, expression levels
extractions.baseline: Previous estimate by Hu et al.
extractions.result: 1,600/4,623 genes (34.6%) in y-ome, 111 with absolutely no evidence of function
